reason: Existing actor catalog/runtime/API/frontend/tests/ledgers are present and verified through Phase 5; first execution step generates state artifacts and gap matrices without replacing architecture.
evidence: Phase 5 ledger and release gate present

reason: Generated runtime state artifacts parse as valid JSON/JSONL, git diff check returned no errors, and catalog state check confirmed 27,753 actors plus expected base-family counts.

The existing BaseActorRunner is the right inherited trait point for all actor copies. Added opt-in knowledge lookup/record behavior, freshness decisions, bounded graph traversal policy, and contract tests without changing default no-store execution semantics.

The actor run API already persists task metadata and is the correct surface for exposing runtime knowledge decisions. Added top-level knowledge/runtime_metadata serialization, request options forwarding for knowledge_context, and a regression that detail fetch preserves metadata.
The runtime already has cost tracking and tracing primitives, so D1 adds actor-specific governance metadata at the shared runner layer: trace, security assessment, cost estimate, and quality eval.

User correctly identified that API-first/provider-first was implicit but not forceful enough in the prompt/R&D/gap docs. Updated execution docs to require official/public APIs, provider SDKs, existing connectors, and platform APIs before scrape/browser/new implementation, and added B2 provider-ladder map as a pending gate.
evidence: Final execution prompt now states API-first/provider-first mandatory rule
External R&D now includes API-first platform spine section
Gap matrix now includes API-first/provider-first routing row

reason: User requested the prompt be made 100% one-shot SaaS-ready. Codex added mandatory one-shot continuous execution, explicit simultaneous multi-agent coding/gap-fix lanes, lane-aware result packet fields, and non-deferrable release readiness criteria. Claude pass 2 returned PASS with no blocking findings and no fixbacks required.
evidence: Simultaneous multi-agent coding and gap-fix execution section added
Result packet schema now includes lane_id, lane_type, parallel_safe, lock_conflicts_checked

reason: User required the SaaS to leave room for future extension across any workflow category or platform. Codex added an explicit extensible workflow substrate contract and B3 implementation gate requiring specs/adapters/profiles/UI/API/QA contracts and no-core-rewrite proof before accepting future categories. Claude pass 2 confirmed the sequencing gate closes the timing risk.
evidence: Final prompt now requires extensible workflow platform structure
External R&D now includes category-agnostic extension point section
Gap matrix now includes extensible workflow substrate row
B3 must be accepted before any new platform-category packet beyond Phase 5 base families

reason: Existing ActorSpec.ProviderStep and family builder were the right place to encode API-first/provider-first order. Added provider tiers, connector references, rationales, unsupported-route ladders, tests, and provider ladder documentation without replacing the native runtime.
evidence: catalog provider ladder recompute loaded 27,753 actors

reason: Existing actor runtime contracts are the correct inheritance point for future workflow categories. Added WorkflowSpec, ProviderLadder, WorkflowAdapter, WorkflowProfile, WorkflowUIContract, WorkflowAPISurface, WorkflowQAGate, and WorkflowRegistry so future categories can register without shared runtime/router/storage rewrites.

Existing stdio MCP server was the right surface for agent access. Added actor_search, actor_get, actor_route, and actor_run tools backed by the local catalog and native runtime, with unsupported routes blocked locally instead of delegating to Apify.

Existing actor runtime, eval, security, and WorkflowProfile contracts are the right inheritance point for self-learning. Added sanitized learning events, replay-gated strategy patch proposals, profile promotion rules, and runner metadata without enabling autonomous unsafe profile mutation.

Existing actor trace/eval/security metadata is the right source for deterministic regression fixture candidates. Added redacted trace-to-fixture candidate generation without automatically writing or promoting fixtures.
